description_long: This object is the core of an apple that has been mostly eaten, with only the central fibrous core and a small amount of flesh remaining. The surface is irregular and uneven, with visible bite marks and areas where the skin has been removed, exposing the pale, slightly browned interior. The top retains a small portion of the reddish-yellow apple skin, while the rest of the object is a mix of off-white, yellow, and brown hues, indicating oxidation and aging. The core is vertically elongated and slightly twisted, with remnants of the apple's natural curvature. There are no visible seeds, but the central cavity and fibrous texture are apparent. The object appears organic, soft, and moist, with a rough, uneven finish and some discoloration from exposure.
description: A mostly eaten apple core with reddish-yellow skin at the top and a rough, browned, fibrous surface.
description_view_0: Shows the apple core's side with prominent bite marks and a small patch of red skin at the top.
description_view_1: Displays a flatter side with more exposed, browned flesh and less visible skin.
description_view_2: Highlights the central core with a mix of red skin and oxidized, rough interior.
description_view_3: Reveals the top opening and deep bite marks, with the most visible browning and irregularity.
attribute_visible_damage_or_blemishes: browning, bite marks, and surface bruising
